title: New Mexico
excerpt:
Consumer Assistance in New Mexico

The New Mexico Consumer Assistance Program is run by the New Mexico Office of the Superintendent of Insurance. Staff can answer questions about health insurance regulations, rights, and responsibilities. They can help you file a complaint or appeal a health plan decision. Get consumer help with a problem or question.

Medicaid in New Mexico

You can apply for Medicaid in New Mexico by contacting your state Medicaid agency.

SHOP Health Insurance Marketplace® in New Mexico

If your small business is located in New Mexico, you can use the New Mexico Health Insurance Exchange website to learn more about offering your employees health coverage.

SHOP Health Insurance Marketplace® for Employees in New Mexico

If your employer offers SHOP health coverage in New Mexico, you should contact your employer or visit the New Mexico Health Insurance Exchange website for more information.

Health Insurance Marketplace® in New Mexico

If you live in New Mexico, you’ll use the beWellnm website to enroll in health coverage.

CHIP in New Mexico

New MexiKids covers uninsured children in families with moderate incomes that are too high to qualify for Medicaid. You can find out whether your children qualify for New MexiKids right now. Fill out a Marketplace application and find out whether your children qualify. Learn more about New MexiKids.
